svg.text: Prerequisite: Output format stable → Reward computable

text.font-family: Arial, 'Helvetica Neue', Helvetica, 'PingFang SC', 'Microsoft YaHei', sans-serif

svg.text: Breakthrough: Discover new strategies beyond SFT demonstrations

text.font-family: Arial, 'Helvetica Neue', Helvetica, 'PingFang SC', 'Microsoft YaHei', sans-serif

svg.text: ✓ Stable format + Strategy generalization = Deployment ready

text.font-family: Arial, 'Helvetica Neue', Helvetica, 'PingFang SC', 'Microsoft YaHei', sans-serif

svg.text: Why can't we skip SFT and go directly to RL?

text.font-family: Arial, 'Helvetica Neue', Helvetica, 'PingFang SC', 'Microsoft YaHei', sans-serif

svg.text: Base model output is unstructured → Cannot parse JSON → Reward function returns NaN → Gradients all zero → Training completely fails

text.font-family: Arial, 'Helvetica Neue', Helvetica, 'PingFang SC', 'Microsoft YaHei', sans-serif

svg.text: SFT memorizes distribution → RL generalizes strategy

text.font-family: Arial, 'Helvetica Neue', Helvetica, 'PingFang SC', 'Microsoft YaHei', sans-serif

svg.text: SFT: max Σ log P(y|x) (fit training distribution) RL: max E[R(τ)] (optimize task objective)

text.font-family: Arial, 'Helvetica Neue', Helvetica, 'PingFang SC', 'Microsoft YaHei', sans-serif

svg.text: When "no matter how many demonstrations are added, new scenarios still perform poorly" → Tipping point to switch to RL
